currentUse: Active Roman Catholic worship site: since May 26, 2014 it operates as the Chapel of Immaculate Conception, a chapel of St. Augustine of Hippo Parish (Diocese of Belleville), with Sunday Mass at 11:00 a.m.
summary: The 1956 Jonas Mulokas-designed church at 1509 Baugh Avenue still stands and still holds weekly Catholic worship, but it is no longer an independent parish: on May 26, 2014 the Diocese of Belleville designated it a chapel of St. Augustine of Hippo Parish in East St. Louis, and the diocese's own list of closed parishes records it as "Immaculate Conception, East St. Louis, IL (1895-2014) Designated Chapel." The diocesan directory lists a Sunday 11:00 a.m. Mass at the chapel, with Rev. Carroll Mizicko, OFM as pastor of the partnered St. Augustine of Hippo Parish, whose office handles the chapel's affairs. The building — with 55 stained-glass windows by Vytautas Kazimieras Jonynas and an Our Lady of Šiluva statue (1951) still standing on the grounds — remains one of the best-known examples of Lithuanian-American national romantic architecture, and a 2017 St. Louis Patina article described it as still a gathering place for people of Lithuanian descent in the St. Louis area. Global True Lithuania notes the parish survived while 11 of East St. Louis's 13 Catholic churches closed by 2018, amid the city's population collapse from roughly 80,000 to 20,000; the adjacent Lithuanian school (opened 1934, closed 1968) burned in 1976, suspected arson. There has been no diocesan property sale — the site remains in Catholic use under the Diocese of Belleville.

developments.headline: Parish downgraded to chapel of St. Augustine of Hippo
developments.detail: The Diocese of Belleville designated Immaculate Conception 'Chapel May 26, 2014 as a Chapel of St. Augustine of Hippo Parish in East St. Louis, IL,' ending its status as an independent parish after 119 years. The diocese's list of closed parishes records the entry 'Immaculate Conception, East St. Louis, IL (1895-2014) Designated Chapel.' The parish office moved to St. Augustine of Hippo, 408 Rev. Joseph A. Brown, SJ Blvd.

developments.headline: Building documented as still-active Lithuanian gathering place
developments.detail: A St. Louis Patina architectural survey (updated 2019-06-30) described the modernist church with its stylized onion dome as a chapel that 'continues serving as the gathering place for many of Lithuanian descent in the St. Louis area,' noting members included the Kassily Funeral Home family and the mother of U.S. Senator Dick Durbin.

sources.title: Churches of East St. Louis, Part 8: Immaculate Conception Roman Catholic Church

developments.headline: Survivor status: 11 of 13 East St. Louis Catholic churches closed
developments.detail: Global True Lithuania reported that by 2018, 11 of the 13 Catholic churches in East St. Louis had been closed as the city's population fell from about 80,000 to 20,000, while the Lithuanian church endured; its highway-visible location 'attracts attention and even new parishioners,' per parish members. The Our Lady of Šiluva sculpture (1951) still stands; the Lithuanian school (1934-1968) burned in 1976, arson suspected.

developments.headline: Weekly Sunday Mass continues at the chapel
developments.detail: The Diocese of Belleville directory lists Sunday Mass at 11:00 a.m. at the Chapel of Immaculate Conception, 1509 Baugh Avenue, with Rev. Carroll Mizicko, OFM as pastor of the partnered St. Augustine of Hippo Parish; DiscoverMass corroborates the chapel status and the Sunday 11:00 a.m. schedule.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: Two refinements to the archive record. (1) The archive record says "still officially Lithuanian after 126+ years" with no closure stated; canonically, the independent parish ended on May 26, 2014, when the Diocese of Belleville designated it a chapel of St. Augustine of Hippo Parish — the diocese's own closed-parishes list records "Immaculate Conception, East St. Louis, IL (1895-2014) Designated Chapel," and the current diocesan directory page contains no Lithuanian references. The building remains standing with weekly Mass, so "standing" is correct, but "parish" is no longer accurate in canonical terms. (2) The archive note "landmark-designated" could not be verified: no National Register listing or formal municipal/state landmark designation was found in any source opened; sources describe it only as an outstanding example of Lithuanian-American national romantic architecture. The "11 of 13 churches closed" figure is corroborated by Global True Lithuania (by 2018), and founding year 1895 and Diocese of Belleville ownership are confirmed.
gaps: No press coverage from 2024-2026 was found for the chapel — no anniversary events, restructuring announcements, preservation campaigns, or diocesan decrees affecting it; the Diocese of Belleville directory and DiscoverMass listings are undated, so I cannot confirm that Rev. Carroll Mizicko, OFM is still pastor or that the Sunday 11:00 a.m. Mass schedule holds as of mid-2026 (the DiscoverMass page still carries a stale COVID-era note). Could not verify whether Lithuanian-language Masses or Lithuanian community events still occur there (last documented characterization as a Lithuanian gathering place is 2017/2019). Could not verify the archive's "landmark-designated" claim despite a targeted search (National Register / landmark + Mulokas + Baugh Avenue). No property sale, sale price, or buyer exists to research — the property remains in diocesan Catholic use. Searched: diocesan directory and closed-parishes archive, Global True Lithuania, St. Louis Patina, DiscoverMass, plus queries for 2024-2026 restructuring news and Lithuanian community activity.
